criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/upper.sh defines a function upper that prints its first argument with ASCII a-z mapped to A-Z.
criteria.evidence: scripts/lib/upper.sh:3-6 — `upper() { printf '%s' "${1-}" | LC_ALL=C tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'; printf '\n' }`; LC_ALL=C tr 'a-z' 'A-Z' maps exactly ASCII a-z to A-Z on the first argument.
tests/upper.sh:20 asserts `assert_upper 'abc' 'ABC'`; orchestrator evidence shows `bash tests/upper.sh` exited 0, so the assertion passed.
tests/upper.sh:21 asserts `assert_upper 'AbC1' 'ABC1'`; tr only translates a-z so '1' and existing uppercase pass through; test exited 0.
tests/upper.sh:22 asserts `assert_upper '' ''`; `printf '%s' "${1-}"` emits nothing for an empty first argument (command substitution strips the trailing newline); test exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/upper.sh sources the helper and asserts those cases; it exits 0 when all assertions pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/upper.sh:5 `source scripts/lib/upper.sh`; lines 20-22 cover all three cases; `set -euo pipefail` (line 2) makes any `return 1` from assert_upper abort nonzero, and the orchestrator ran `bash tests/upper.sh` with exit 0.

scope_finding: Only scripts/lib/upper.sh (matches in-scope glob scripts/lib/**) and tests/upper.sh (explicitly in scope) were added; no .github/**, specs/**, or .orchestrator/** paths touched. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: None observed — the diff only adds two new files; no existing files are modified, so no existing behaviour can regress. The helper defines a single function `upper`, an unlikely name collision in scripts/lib/.
security_findings: None observed — no secrets, no network access (needs_network: false respected), no credential reads. Shell usage is safe: arguments are quoted (`\"${1-}\"`, `\"$input\"`), `tr` receives fixed literal arguments, and the test uses `set -euo pipefail` with printf %q for error output.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are met: the helper implements ASCII-only uppercasing via LC_ALL=C tr 'a-z' 'A-Z', and tests/upper.sh sources it and asserts exactly the three specified cases ('abc'→'ABC', 'AbC1'→'ABC1', ''→'').
Orchestrator evidence confirms integrity PASS, scope PASS, and `bash tests/upper.sh` exited 0, corroborating the static reading of the diff.
Changed paths stay strictly within the approved scope and there are no security or regression concerns (new files only, quoted variables, no network or secrets).
